description: Primary Button Gradient - Add a red-orange to orange diagonal gradient to all primary action buttons across the web app

userStories.title: Define gradient CSS class
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable CSS class that applies the primary gradient and hover state so I can use it across all buttons without duplicating styles.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `.btn-primary-gradient` class in `apps/web/src/app/globals.css`
Normal state background: `linear-gradient(135deg, #FF6B35 0%, #FF9500 100%)` (red-orange to primary orange, diagonal)
Hover state background: darkened gradient `linear-gradient(135deg, #E55A2B 0%, #E08600 100%)`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply gradient to dashboard buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Start Workout button and other dashboard action buttons to have the gradient so they feel more vibrant and engaging.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop 'Start Workout' button in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx` uses `btn-primary-gradient` class instead of `bg-primary` + `hover:bg-orange-600`
Mobile 'Start Workout' button (fixed bottom bar) uses `btn-primary-gradient`
Workout type modal 'Start Workout' submit button uses `btn-primary-gradient`
'Accept' button on burn buddy requests uses `btn-primary-gradient`
'Join Workout' inline button uses `btn-primary-gradient`
'Add Burn Buddy' and 'Add Burn Squad' icon Link elements use `btn-primary-gradient`
All `bg-primary` + `hover:bg-orange-600` pairs in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx` are replaced
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Apply gradient to friends page buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Add Friend and Send Burn Buddy Request buttons on the friends page to match the new gradient style.
acceptanceCriteria: '+ Add Friend' button in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/friends/page.tsx` uses `btn-primary-gradient` instead of `bg-primary` + `hover:bg-orange-600`
'Send Request' (burn buddy) button uses `btn-primary-gradient`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Apply gradient to burn buddy detail and creation pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Save and Send buttons on burn buddy pages to use the gradient.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Save Schedule' button in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-buddies/[id]/page.tsx` uses `btn-primary-gradient` instead of `bg-primary` + `hover:bg-orange-600`
'Send Request' button in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-buddies/new/page.tsx` uses `btn-primary-gradient`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Apply gradient to profile page buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Accept and Request Burn Buddy buttons on profile pages to use the gradient.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Accept' button in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/profile/[uid]/page.tsx` uses `btn-primary-gradient` instead of `bg-primary` + `hover:bg-orange-600`
'Request Burn Buddy' button on the same page uses `btn-primary-gradient`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Apply gradient to account page buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Save and toggle buttons on the account page to use the gradient.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Save' (username) button in `apps/web/src/app/(main)/account/page.tsx` uses `btn-primary-gradient` instead of `bg-primary` + `hover:bg-orange-600`
'Hide/Re-enable Getting Started' button uses `btn-primary-gradient` in its orange state (keep `bg-success hover:bg-green-600` for the green state)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Apply gradient to Firebase Auth widget buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Sign In and Continue buttons on the login page to match the gradient style.
acceptanceCriteria: In `apps/web/src/app/globals.css`, update `.firebaseui-form-actions .mdl-button--raised.mdl-button--colored` to use `background: linear-gradient(135deg, #FF6B35 0%, #FF9500 100%) !important` instead of flat `background-color`
Hover state uses `background: linear-gradient(135deg, #E55A2B 0%, #E08600 100%) !important`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
